summary: Fix querySkills() blocker #2: replace hardcoded .slice(0,3) and .slice(0,5) caps with configurable DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS=10 and ABSOLUTE_MAX_RESULTS=20. Enables more diverse skill recommendations while preventing context window explosion.

added: Static constants: DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS=10, SOURCE_RATIO=0.6, ABSOLUTE_MAX_RESULTS=20 at lines 185-187
Optional second parameter to querySkills(): { maxResults = DEFAULT_MAX_RESULTS } = {} at line 198
Proportional per-source capping: Math.ceil(effectiveMax * SOURCE_RATIO) at lines 206, 212, 221
Absolute ceiling enforcement: Math.min(maxResults, ABSOLUTE_MAX_RESULTS) at line 203
4 new test cases in selection.test.js covering default cap, custom limits, and absolute ceiling
modified: querySkills() signature now accepts optional options parameter
Per-source cap calculation uses proportional formula instead of hardcoded 3
Final slice uses effectiveMax instead of hardcoded 5

notes: TDD cycle: RED (tests written first) → GREEN (implementation) → REFACTOR (extracted constants). All 4 new tests pass. Backward compatible: existing caller at index.js:237 uses default maxResults=10 (up from hardcoded 5). No regressions in skill-rl-manager test suite.
